description_long: This object is a large, angular solar energy installation featuring multiple tilted photovoltaic panel arrays mounted on a broad, stepped platform. The structure includes two prominent solar panel sections, each set at a distinct angle, likely to maximize sunlight capture. The base platform is composed of various geometric surfaces, some of which are raised or recessed, and includes a mix of smooth and textured areas. The installation is surrounded by low barriers and has several technical modules or enclosures, possibly for electrical or maintenance purposes. The color palette is primarily gray, black, and blue, with the solar panels displaying a grid-like pattern typical of photovoltaic cells. The overall design suggests a fixed, ground-mounted solar infrastructure suitable for urban or industrial environments.
description: A large, angular solar energy installation with multiple tilted photovoltaic panels on a stepped, geometric platform, featuring technical modules and low barriers in gray, black, and blue.
description_view_0: Side view showing the two main tilted solar panel arrays and the stepped, multi-level platform with technical modules and barriers.
description_view_1: Frontal view highlighting the prominent tilt of the solar panels and the arrangement of technical enclosures at the rear.
description_view_2: Opposite side view revealing the geometric complexity of the platform and the spatial relationship between the two main solar panel arrays.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the back of the solar panels and the platform's surface textures, with barriers and technical modules visible.
attribute_panel_orientation: Panels are tilted at approximately 30-35 degrees, facing toward the front (view 1), with a fixed orientation.
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with an angular, stepped platform and visible aluminum or steel framing.
attribute_array_configuration: Two main arrays, each consisting of a cluster of panels arranged in parallel, separated by a central platform section.
